metadata.title: What's a Beat Sheet?
metadata.audience: Humanitarians AI Fellows learning to use the Brutalist pipeline

beats.narration_text: What's a Beat Sheet? Audio-first — narration is the master clock. Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: What is a beat_sheet.json and how does it control the timing of a Brutalist video?
output: Each beat has: id, narration, shot, duration
Audio is generated first — duration is measured
All visuals conform to measured audio length

beats.narration_text: The beat sheet is the heart of every video. It's a JSON file with one entry per beat — a cold open, a centerpiece visual, supporting scenes, a verdict, a handoff, and an outro. Each beat has narration text, a shot type, and a duration. The duration comes from the measured MP3, not from an estimate.

Here's what audio-first means in practice. Claude authors the narration text, then generates the MP3. It measures the actual playback time — say, eleven point seven seconds. That measured duration becomes the on-screen duration for that beat. The Remotion scene is rendered to exactly that length. Never a second longer or shorter.

props.title: audio-first
props.code:
# 1. Narration text written:
"The finding was an 18% improvement…"

# 2. Kokoro generates mp3/beat-B02.mp3
# 3. ffprobe measures: 11.7s

# 4. Remotion renders B02 at:
#    durationInFrames = 11.7 * 30 = 351 frames

beats.narration_text: You don't have to write the beat sheet yourself. Claude writes it from your prompt. But you should be able to read it. If a beat's narration is wrong, you can edit the text and re-generate just that beat's audio. The shot type tells you which Remotion scene or Manim animation will run.

artifactLines: beat_id: B00 through B06 — the beat sequence.
narration_text: what Liam says — edit here to revise.
shot.remotion.pattern: the scene component to render.
actual_duration_s: measured from the generated MP3.
props.sparkLine: Read it. Edit it. Rebuild it.

beats.narration_text: Audio-first: narration is the master clock. Every visual duration conforms to the measured MP3.

artifactLines: beat_sheet.json is the heart of every Brutalist video.
One entry per beat: narration, shot, duration.
Audio is generated first — actual duration is measured.
Every visual conforms to that duration. Never hand-fix timing.

beats.narration_text: Here's the prompt. Open my beat_sheet.json and explain what each field controls. If I want to change the narration in beat B02, what's the exact edit to make and what command re-generates just that beat's audio? Run that prompt with your beat sheet open, and you'll understand the whole structure in one pass. Episode eight covers voices — that's next.

props.command: Open my beat_sheet.json and explain what each field controls. If I want to change the narration in beat B02, what's the exact edit and what command re-generates just that beat's audio?

beats.narration_text: What's a Beat Sheet. Episode seven of twelve. Next: Voices.
